title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Estonia
description: Estonia's same-sex marriage law, passed by parliament in 2023, entered into force on 1 January 2024, making Estonia the first Baltic state where same-sex couples can marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil union law takes effect in Latvia
description: Latvia's civil union legislation entered into force in 2024, giving same-sex couples access to legal partnership recognition for the first time in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dominica decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 2024, consensual same-sex sexual activity ceased to be a criminal offence in Dominica after the High Court struck down the colonial-era provisions of the Sexual Offences Act.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Namibia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Namibia's High Court declared the common-law offences of sodomy and unnatural sexual offences unconstitutional in 2024, ending the criminalization of consensual sex between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Niue decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: The Pacific island nation of Niue removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex sexual activity in 2024 as part of a reform of its criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iraq criminalizes same-sex relations
description: In 2024, Iraq's parliament passed legislation imposing prison terms for same-sex relations and for gender transition, formally criminalizing homosexuality in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mali criminalizes homosexuality
description: Mali adopted legislation in 2024 making homosexuality a criminal offence, reversing its previous status as one of the West African states without an explicit ban.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luanne Peterpaul enters New Jersey legislature as its first out lesbian member
description: In 2024, Luanne Peterpaul took office in the New Jersey General Assembly, becoming the first out lesbian to serve in the state's legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Huang Jie becomes first openly gay member of Taiwan's Legislative Yuan
description: Huang Jie won a seat in Taiwan's 2024 legislative elections, making her the first openly gay person to serve in the Legislative Yuan.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gabriel Attal becomes first openly gay Prime Minister of France
description: Gabriel Attal was appointed Prime Minister of France on 9 January 2024, the first openly gay person to hold the office and the eighth openly LGBT head of government in Europe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Danica Roem serves in both chambers of a state legislature, a first for a trans person
description: After earlier terms in Virginia's House of Delegates, Danica Roem took her seat in the Virginia State Senate in 2024, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to and serving in both chambers of a US state legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Monica Márquez becomes first openly gay Chief Justice of Colorado Supreme Court
description: In 2024, Justice Monica Márquez assumed the role of Chief Justice of the Colorado Supreme Court, the first openly gay person to lead the court.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colman Domingo earns Oscar nomination as openly gay American playing a gay role
description: Colman Domingo's 2024 Academy Award nomination for his lead performance in Rustin made him the first openly gay American man nominated for an Oscar for portraying a gay character.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Asher HaVon wins The Voice as first openly LGBTQ contestant to take the title
description: Singer Asher HaVon won the US television competition The Voice in 2024, the first openly LGBTQ contestant to win the show.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Karla Sofía Gascón is first openly trans actor to win a major Cannes prize
description: At the 2024 Cannes Film Festival, Karla Sofía Gascón shared the Best Actress award with her Emilia Pérez costars Selena Gomez, Adriana Paz and Zoe Saldaña, becoming the first openly transgender actor to win a major prize at Cannes.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pride flag raised over Los Angeles City Hall for the first time
description: In 2024, the pride flag flew above Los Angeles City Hall for the first time in the city's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bailey Anne Kennedy crowned Miss Maryland USA as first openly trans titleholder
description: Bailey Anne Kennedy won the Miss Maryland USA pageant in 2024, becoming the first openly transgender woman to hold the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nava Mau earns first Emmy supporting-actress nomination for an openly trans woman
description: Nava Mau was nominated for the 2024 Primetime Emmy for supporting actress in a limited or anthology series or movie, the first openly transgender woman recognized in that category.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrew Mortensen completes round-the-world cycle as first openly gay man to do so
description: Texan cyclist Andrew Mortensen finished a non-continuous circumnavigation of the globe by bicycle in 2024, having started in 2020, and became the first openly gay man officially recorded to cycle around the world.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stacie Gregory becomes Sausalito's first openly lesbian police chief
description: In 2024, Stacie Gregory took over as police chief of Sausalito, California, the first openly LGBTQ+ person and first openly lesbian officer to lead the department.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Manvi Madhu Kashyap becomes India's first openly transgender police sub-inspector
description: In 2024, Manvi Madhu Kashyap was appointed as a police sub-inspector, the first openly transgender person to reach that rank in India.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Liechtenstein
description: Liechtenstein's same-sex marriage law entered into force on 1 January 2025, allowing same-sex couples in the principality to marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Thailand
description: Thailand's marriage equality law came into force in January 2025, making it the first country in Southeast Asia where same-sex couples can legally marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lithuania's Constitutional Court rules on civil unions for same-sex couples
description: In 2025, Lithuania's Constitutional Court issued a ruling on the legal recognition of same-sex civil unions, adding judicial weight to partnership recognition for same-sex couples in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Burkina Faso criminalizes homosexuality
description: In 2025, Burkina Faso adopted legislation making same-sex relations a criminal offence, introducing an explicit ban where none had previously existed.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trinidad and Tobago re-criminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 2025, an appellate ruling in Trinidad and Tobago restored the criminal status of consensual same-sex acts, reversing the 2018 High Court decision that had struck the ban down.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saint Lucia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 2025, consensual same-sex sexual activity ceased to be a criminal offence in Saint Lucia, removing colonial-era penalties from the island's law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Poland's last LGBT-free zones are abolished
description: In 2025, the final Polish local governments withdrew their so-called LGBT-free zone declarations, ending the policy that at its peak covered around a third of the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sarah McBride becomes first openly transgender member of the US House
description: Sarah McBride of Delaware was sworn into the US House of Representatives in January 2025, the first openly transgender person to serve in the chamber.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Niger criminalizes homosexuality
description: In 2026, Niger introduced criminal penalties for same-sex relations, adding an explicit ban to its law where none had previously existed.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rob Jetten becomes first openly gay Prime Minister of the Netherlands
description: In 2026, Rob Jetten took office as Prime Minister of the Netherlands, the first openly gay person to lead a Dutch government and the ninth openly LGBT head of government in Europe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
